title: review-findings@1 — structured code-review findings
description: TASK-IMP-112. One record per finding, emitted as review-findings.json beside code-review.md. The markdown stays the human artefact (§1.5); this array is what a machine counts, routes, and acts on. An empty array is valid and is exactly what a zero-finding review emits (§1.6).

file.description: Path as it appeared in the reviewed diff. For a deleted file, the path as it was in the diff — the record is about the diff, not the tree. Recorded as data; never interpolated or executed.

line.description: The first line the finding bears on. A finding spanning a range records the first line here and describes the range in `summary` (a schema that models ranges before anyone needs them is gold-plating).

severity.description: Three-value taxonomy (§1.2). Not the High/Medium/Low prose — a nit and a severe finding are different kinds of thing, and a reviewer who cannot say 'this is a nit' says nothing instead.

clause_ref.description: Names the reviewed task's §1 clause the finding bears on (e.g. "§1.3"), or null when the finding is outside the spec's clauses (§1.3). null is a real category; a fabricated reference is never a substitute for it.

summary.description: What the finding is — the same issue the prose packet describes.

suggested_fix.description: The concrete change proposed to resolve the finding.
